author: mysticryuujin
content:
For the gas, can we just update the client config in the json-rpc compact tests to set the same value on Nethermind?

For blockValue, I don't know off the top of my head

I think slotNumber it's probably not intentional, would have to double check.

And I would have to double check but I think the mempool test is SpecOnly so it just has to match the format/fields, not perfect match the values.

author: newmanifold000
content:
Yes, we can update the client config. Should that go directly in hive's client config, or in also forkenv.json? Since this only affects rpc-compat tests, forkenv.json seems like the right place.

For blockValue, geth passes 0 for buildBlock tests [here](https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/miner/payload_building.go) so the generated fixtures all have 0.

Oh and the mempool test doesn't have the speconly comment

author: mysticryuujin
content: I think there's a client config file in the rpc-compat folder

author: newmanifold000
content:
Thanks! Is there anything i can do here to help? 

Also `SlotNumber` issue seems to be because geth shares the same struct `ExecutableData`  and doesn't omit when its null but that shouldn't exist for ExecutionPayloadV3 (pre glamsterdam)

author: mysticryuujin
content: We can probably update it to omit null, we'll have to check if V4 can omit or it needs more changes on the geth side.

author: newmanifold000
content: I think If amsterdam is active then field shouldn't be null in first place so adding omitempty should be fine, I am not that familier with geth's codebase so probably you can confirm on that later.
